<findings>

[observation] [warning] 16 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
[observation] [warning] 16 entry points is unusually broad for a single zone; audit whether all exports need to be public or if the public API surface can be narrowed.
[observation] [info] High cohesion (0.66) and low coupling (0.34) indicate a well-bounded analysis domain — this is the healthiest zone in the batch.
[observation] [info] claude-client.ts should proxy through @n-dx/llm-client to maintain the four-tier dependency hierarchy and avoid direct vendor coupling.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "analysis-engine" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [info] The zone name 'analysis-engine' is singular and bounded but the zone spans 9 directories with 16 entry points; rename or split the zone so the name truthfully describes its scope — a misleading name causes developers to underestimate blast radius when modifying files here
[suggestion] [info] completion-reader.ts handles streaming completion I/O while its sibling files perform domain analysis — extract streaming/I/O adapters into a dedicated adapter layer below the analysis services to give I/O concerns a stable, separately evolvable home
[relationship] [critical] analysis-engine → cli-operations (12 imports): domain layer importing from CLI layer is a hard layer inversion; analysis services must not depend on CLI command modules — extract shared types or utilities downward to break this coupling
[anti-pattern] [warning] 16 entry points spanning 9 directories means there is no canonical import path into this zone; consumers import from arbitrary internal paths, making any file move a breaking change — consolidate exports behind a single index or public.ts to create a stable zone API surface
[anti-pattern] [info] enrich-config.ts (classified [config]) is co-located with domain services in analysis-engine; configuration files that change on deployment cadence should not share a zone with algorithmic services that change on feature cadence — extract to a dedicated config layer to decouple change rates

</findings>

<insights>

- 16 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- With 16 entry points this zone has a wide public surface — consider whether all are truly public API or if some should be internal
- High cohesion (0.66) confirms this zone is well-clustered around a single domain responsibility
- The claude-client.ts inside this zone suggests a direct LLM dependency — verify it routes through @n-dx/llm-client rather than calling Anthropic APIs directly
- High cohesion (0.66) and low coupling (0.34) indicate a well-bounded analysis domain — this is the healthiest zone in the batch.
- 16 entry points is unusually broad for a single zone; audit whether all exports need to be public or if the public API surface can be narrowed.
- claude-client.ts should proxy through @n-dx/llm-client to maintain the four-tier dependency hierarchy and avoid direct vendor coupling.
- Zone "analysis-engine" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- The 12 outgoing imports from analysis-engine into cli-operations constitute a layer inversion — domain analysis code must not depend on CLI modules; this is the most severe structural violation in the batch
- analysis-engine → cli-operations (12 imports): domain layer importing from CLI layer is a hard layer inversion; analysis services must not depend on CLI command modules — extract shared types or utilities downward to break this coupling
- enrich-config.ts is classified as [config] inside analysis-engine, mixing pipeline configuration with domain service logic in the same zone — configuration for the enrichment pipeline should be co-located with other config artifacts or extracted to prevent config churn from touching the domain zone
- The 16-entry-point surface combined with files spanning 9 directories means analysis-engine's public API cannot be audited by reading a single public.ts or index.ts — callers must know internal paths to import correctly, defeating the purpose of a zone boundary
- enrich-config.ts (classified [config]) is co-located with domain services in analysis-engine; configuration files that change on deployment cadence should not share a zone with algorithmic services that change on feature cadence — extract to a dedicated config layer to decouple change rates
- 16 entry points spanning 9 directories means there is no canonical import path into this zone; consumers import from arbitrary internal paths, making any file move a breaking change — consolidate exports behind a single index or public.ts to create a stable zone API surface
- completion-reader.ts handles streaming I/O concerns at the same zone level as algorithmic services — an implicit I/O abstraction layer is missing between the LLM client and the analysis pipeline
- The zone name 'analysis-engine' implies a single engine, but files span 9 directories with 16 entry points — the name sets a false expectation of bounded scope that misleads consumers about the zone's actual surface area
- completion-reader.ts handles streaming completion I/O while its sibling files perform domain analysis — extract streaming/I/O adapters into a dedicated adapter layer below the analysis services to give I/O concerns a stable, separately evolvable home
- The zone name 'analysis-engine' is singular and bounded but the zone spans 9 directories with 16 entry points; rename or split the zone so the name truthfully describes its scope — a misleading name causes developers to underestimate blast radius when modifying files here
- [call graph] 998 internal calls, 94 outgoing, 210 incoming (cohesion: 0.91, coupling: 0.09)

</insights>
